intro: Microliabum polymnioides, also known as Mexican fleabane, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, commonly found in the mountainous regions of Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its reported antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Microliabum polymnioides include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Mexican fleabane include teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as topical creams and ointments used to treat skin conditions and wounds.
